perturbation.original_text: 7 NON-SOLICITATION OF EMPL OYEES The Receiving Party agrees that , save as contemplated in any definitive transaction agreement concluded between the Parties for purposes of the Transaction, it will not, for a period of 24 months a fter the Signature Date, directly or indirectly, and on its own behalf or on behalf of any other person, encourage, entice, induce or persuade any person - 7.1 who is, as at the Signature Date or during the course of the Parties discussions relating to the Pu rpose, in the employ of the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates ; and 7.2 with whom the Receiving Party has had or may have had any contact in relation to the Confidential Information or whose name and/or any other details are included in the Confidential I nformation, to terminate such employment, nor shall it encourage or support any of its Affiliates to do so.
perturbation.changed_text: 7 NON-SOLICITATION OF EMPL OYEES The Receiving Party agrees that , save as contemplated in any definitive transaction agreement concluded between the Parties for purposes of the Transaction, it will try not to, for a reasonable period a fter the Signature Date, directly or indirectly, and on its own behalf or on behalf of any other person, actively encourage, directly entice, forcefully induce or strongly persuade any person - 7.1 who is, as at the Signature Date or during the course of the Parties discussions relating to the Pu rpose, in the employ of the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates ; and 7.2 with whom the Receiving Party has had only superficial contact in relation to the Confidential Information or whose readily available name and/or easily accessible other details are included in the Confidential I nformation, to terminate such employment, nor will it encourage or support any of its Affiliates to do so.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'it will not' to 'it will try not to' weakens the obligation. Replacing '24 months' with 'a reasonable period' introduces ambiguity. Adding qualifiers like 'actively,' 'directly,' 'forcefully,' and 'strongly' makes the prohibition less absolute. Changing 'any contact' to 'only superficial contact' and adding 'readily available name' and 'easily accessible other details' narrows the scope of the non-solicitation agreement in a vague manner.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 186(2)(b) of the Labour Relations Act defines unfair labour practice as 'the unfair suspension of an employee or any other unfair disciplinary action short of dismissal in respect of an employee'. A broadly interpreted non-solicitation agreement could potentially restrict an employee's freedom to seek employment elsewhere, indirectly impacting their labour rights. By weakening the original obligation and making it ambiguous, the modified text creates uncertainty about the extent to which the Receiving Party must refrain from actions that might influence an employee's decision to leave their current employment. The 'reasonable period' clause may not be honored by the South African Labour court, as a period must be specifically laid out to be enforceable.

perturbation.justification: The modification introduces ambiguities that undermine the enforceability of the non-solicitation agreement, potentially conflicting with fair labour practices. The original text provided a clear 24-month restriction; the changed text replaces this with 'a reasonable period,' which is vague and open to interpretation, impacting the certainty required in contractual obligations. The altered language weakens the original obligation, creating uncertainty about the receiving party's commitment to refrain from actions that could influence an employee's decision to leave their current employment. The Law Explanation states 'The 'reasonable period' clause may not be honored by the South African Labour court, as a period must be specifically laid out to be enforceable,' indicating a direct contradiction in enforceability. The changes introduce ambiguities making the contract less enforceable than it was. This falls under the 'Ambiguities Legal Contradiction' definition where 'a legal statement is vague, leading to multiple interpretations' and 'an obligation is introduced ambiguously, making it difficult to enforce under state or national law'.
